---
title: "Modèles à variables latentes"
author: "STETSUN Kateryna"
date: "2026-05-2"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


Step 1: Load data
```{r step1, warning=FALSE}
library(dplyr)

data <- read.csv("Datagenus.csv", sep = "\t", dec = ",", header = TRUE)
char_cols <- sapply(data, is.character)
data[char_cols] <- lapply(data[char_cols], as.numeric)
data$forest <- NULL
```

Step 2: Prepare variables for SCGLR

```{r step2}
n <- names(data)
ny <- n[grep("^gen", n)]          # species responses
nx <- n[-grep("^gen", n)]         # all other variables
nx <- nx[!nx %in% c("geology", "surface", "code")]  # offset + additional

# remove empty plots
keep <- rowSums(data[, ny]) > 0
scglr_data <- data[keep, ]
```

Step 3: SCGLR
```{r step3_1, warning=FALSE}
remotes::install_github("SCnext/SCGLR", force = TRUE)
library(SCGLR)

form <- multivariateFormula(ny, c(nx, "I(lat*lon)"), A = c("geology"))
fam  <- rep("poisson", length(ny))

scglr_fit <- scglr(formula = form, data = scglr_data, family = fam, K = 4, offset = scglr_data$surface)

summary(scglr_fit)
screeplot(scglr_fit)
```

Step 4: SCGLR cross-validation with s and l parameters
```{r step4, warning=FALSE}
set.seed(123)
folds <- sample(1:10, nrow(scglr_data), replace = TRUE)

s <- c(0.25, 0.5)
l <- c(1, 2, 4)
design <- expand.grid(s, l)  # all combinations of s and l

K <- 4
rmse <- list()

for(i in 1:nrow(design)){
  rmse[[i]] <- scglrCrossVal(formula = form,
                             data    = scglr_data,
                             family  = fam,
                             K       = K,
                             folds   = folds,
                             offset  = data$surface[keep],
                             method  = methodSR(l = design[i,2], 
                                                s = design[i,1]))
}

# geometric mean of RMSE across all species
rmse_geom <- do.call(cbind, lapply(rmse, function(x) colMeans(log(x))))

# plot
ticksx <- c("с0", paste0("c", 1:K))
labs <- apply(design, 1, function(x) paste("s", x[1], "l", x[2], sep="_"))

matplot(rmse_geom, type = "l", xaxt = "n",
        ylab = "Moyenne géométrique du RMSE",
        xlab = "Nombre de composantes",
        main = "Validation croisée SCGLR - choix de K, s et l")
axis(1, at = seq(1, K+1, by=1), labels = FALSE)
text(seq(1, K+1, by=1) - 0.1, par("usr")[3] - 0.005,
     labels = ticksx, srt = 45, pos = 1, xpd = TRUE)
legend("topright", legend = labs,
       col = 1:ncol(rmse_geom), lty = 1:ncol(rmse_geom),
       cex = 0.8, ncol = 2)

# find best s and l
best <- which.min(rmse_geom[3,])
cat("Best s:", design[best,1], "l:", design[best,2], "\n")
```

Step 5: Refit SCGLR with optimal K=3, s=0.25, l=4
```{r step5_1, warning=FALSE}
# geology must be factor in scglr_data
scglr_data$geology <- as.factor(scglr_data$geology)
# refit
scglr_fit <- scglr(formula = form,
                   data    = scglr_data,
                   family  = fam,
                   K       = 4,
                   offset  = scglr_data$surface,
                   method  = methodSR(s = 0.25, l = 4))
summary(scglr_fit)
screeplot(scglr_fit)

# correlation plots
plot(scglr_fit, plane = c(1,2), threshold = 0.8)
plot(scglr_fit, plane = c(2,3), threshold = 0.8)
plot(scglr_fit, plane = c(1,2), threshold = 0.8, predictor = TRUE)
plot(scglr_fit, plane = c(1,2), threshold = 0.8, predictor = TRUE, factor = "geology")
```

```{r step5_2, warning=FALSE}
# observations colored by geology
plot(scglr_fit, factor = "geology",
     observations = TRUE, pred.arrows = FALSE, covariates = FALSE)
```


Step 6: interpretation
```{r step6_1, warning=FALSE}
# Top env variables per component
u_load <- scglr_fit$u
for(h in 1:3){
  top5 <- rownames(u_load)[order(abs(u_load[,h]), decreasing=TRUE)][1:5]
  cat("SC", h, "driven by:", top5, "\n")
}

# Top species per component
g_load <- scglr_fit$gamma
for(h in 1:3){
  row_h <- g_load[paste0("sc",h), ]
  top3 <- names(sort(abs(row_h), decreasing=TRUE))[1:10]
  cat("SC", h, "explains species:", top3, "\n")
}
```
